content.description: She is finally back for EP 10 of Mercy Me Podcast! Listen in and host Arreona shares whats new in her life. As well as her new goals with career searching, tapping into her passion, and her social life. As always thanks for listening and we hope you enjoy! love you!
content.speaker: Arreona

content.title: Understanding Men?
content.description: In this weeks episode host Arreona kept it short sharing how she is struggling with feelings of guilt. She shares her situation briefly on how people need personal space even when in relationships to grow. She also shares a little bit about understanding the limit and time frame on how much she f
content.speaker: Arreona

content.title: Exposing Myself
content.description: In this weeks episode Arreona admits how she really feels about having a podcast, social media content, and "gender norms". She shares how her life has been recently and resurfaces some goals she neglected. Remember to please follow, leave a comment, and rating. We love and Thank You!
content.speaker: Arreona

content.title: Self Love/Growth
content.description: In this weeks episode host Arreona talks all about self love and how it coincides with self growth. She gives eight examples of what self love really is, and shares ways to implement self love in your life. Arreona also gives examples of what we can do to evolve and develop personally in our live
content.speaker: Arreona

content.title: Do u Judge others? - The Tamil Podcast with me Kanmani💥
content.description: Of course everyone judge others knowing or unknowingly. This podcast give you the reason why we should not judge others.😇 Listen to it and make your day better.Have a Happy day. Love from Kanmani💯
content.speaker: Mercy Nilaa

content.title: Are you Rich? - The Tamil Podcast with me Kanmani❤
content.description: Are you Rich? This podcast will make you to realize that you are rich by heart. Listen to it and tell your reviews. Love Friendship Freedom is there in you💯. Wishing everyone to be rich in heart and happy in mind❤. Love from Kanmani💥
content.speaker: Mercy Nilaa

content.title: LIFE - The Tamil Podcast with me Kanmani.
content.description: This Podcast is all about Life💕 You can find answer in this podcast for the question what is Life?💥Life is explained by a short story. Listen to it and give your feedback. For more intersting stories follow THE TAMIL PODCAST WITH ME KANMANI and share with your Friends.
content.speaker: Mercy Nilaa

content.title: Kobam - The Tamil Podcast with me Kanmani.
content.description: This podcast has an interesting and deep story💥. How do you express your anger. Listen to it and tell your reviews😍.Feel good Podcast.
content.speaker: Mercy Nilaa

Christopher is a husband and father of four. A retired Martin County Sheriff, he now spen

content.title: Third Day Podcast - Episode 1: Steve Morris
content.description:
In this episode I have invited my brother-in-law to help me kick off this new podcast.

Steve Morris has been the Family Pastor at Palm City New Hope in Palm City Florida for over a decade. You can watch his messages online at https://pcnewhope.com.
content.speaker: Rob Austin

content.title: Rooted & Fruitful: Navigating the Spirit vs. the Flesh
content.description: In this episode, Crystle and Jasmine explore the concepts of good and bad fruit as described in the Bible, particularly focusing on the fruits of the Holy Spirit. They discuss the importance of understanding these fruits in relation to one's character and behavior, emphasizing the role of the Hol
content.speaker: Crystle and Jasmine

content.title: The Final Episode - BYYYE!
content.description: This is it, Backseaters. The tires on the minivan are cooked, and she’s retiring to the garage. - Thank you for sending in your stories today as we get to hear all about the ride you’ve taken with us for the past 8 years. We could have done this without you, but then it wouldn’t mean anythi
content.speaker: Dustin Nickerson

content.title: Our Second to Last Episode
content.description: It’s been an amazing 8 years backseaters, but it looks like this minivan of a podcast is finally taking that offramp home. After much consideration we’ve made the decision to pack up the show, this being the second to the last to go. We explain why and spend our time looking back on some of
content.speaker: Dustin Nickerson

content.title: Millennial Nostalgia
content.description: Is Yogurt actually good for you? Does good Vegan Cheese exist? Are we obsessed with Gray? Was Radio more complicated than we remember? There are SO many things to consider about the 90s as we navigate a roller coaster stream of consciousness today on what truly started as a “Choose your own
content.speaker: Dustin Nickerson

content.description: Anjelah Johnson-Reyes dropped her 7th Comedy Special this week ON Mother’s Day, and while an applause and congrats are in order, nothing is going to satisfy the moment more than a bath, for herself, and only her self. content.speaker: Dustin Nickerson

1ऐ भाइवन, अब हम तोहनी के उहे सुभसमाचार बतावऽ हियो जे पहले सुना चुकल हियो, जेकरा तोहनी मानबो कय लऽ हल आउर जेकरा में तोहनी असि्थारो हऽ। 2ओकरे दिया तोहनी के उद्धारो होवऽ हे, जे ऊ सुभसमाचार के जे हम तोहनी के सुनय ली हल इयाद रखऽ

content.title: Colin R. McCulloch, "Sanctified by the Spirit: John Owen, Habits of Grace, and Biblical Counseling" (﻿Reformation Heritage Books, 2024)
content.description: In Sanctified by the Spirit: John Owen, Habits of Grace, and Biblical Counseling (﻿Reformation Heritage Books, 2024) Dr. Colin McCulloch examines how approaches to biblical counseling have diverged over the last generations, proposing John Owen's emphasis on Spirit-infused habitual grace as a helpfu
content.speaker: Marshall Poe

content.title: Brook Wilensky-Lanford, "A God-Shaped Nation: Five Hundred Years of Religion in America" (﻿Atlantic Monthly Press, 2026)
content.description: Ever since conquistadores claimed Taino land in the name of their Catholic God and New England Puritans formed their strictly Protestant “city on a hill,” religion has been central to American life. Even as some found religious freedom—Rhode Island welcomed the Quakers, Jews, and Baptists that Massa
content.speaker: Marshall Poe

content.title: Marinus De Jong, "A Church for a Secular World: The Development of Klaas Schilder's Ecclesiology" (Brill, 2025)
content.description:
The relationship between the Church and the world has been a subject of debate since the Church's earliest days. In ⁠A Church for a Secular World: The Development of Klaas Schilder's Ecclesiology⁠ (Brill, 2025), Marinus De Jong explores how Stanley
 Hauerwas, with his emphasis on the Church as polis
content.speaker: Marshall Poe

content.title: Susanna Drake, "Veiling in the Late Antique World" (Cambridge UP, 2026)
content.description: Veiling meant many things to the ancients. On women, veils could signify virtue, beauty, piety, self-control, and status. On men, covering the head could signify piety or an emotion such as grief. Late Roman mosaics show people covering their hands with veils when receiving or giving something preci
content.speaker: Marshall Poe

content.title: Ayşe Baltacıoğlu-Brammer, "Boundaries of Belonging: Sectarianism and Statecraft in the Early Modern Ottoman Empire" (Cambridge UP, 2026)
content.description:
Examining sectarian divergence in the early modern Middle East, Ayşe 
Baltacıoğlu-Brammer's study provides a fresh perspective on the 
Sunni–Shi'i division. Drawing on Ottoman Turkish, Persian, and European 
sources, Boundaries of Belonging: Sectarianism and Statecraft in the Early Modern Ottoman Em
content.speaker: New Books Network

content.title: Jake Dyble, "Managing Maritime Risk in Early Modern Europe: General Average in Law and Practice in Seventeenth-Century Tuscany" (Boydell Press, 2025)
content.description: Commercial seafaring, both dangerous and with large amounts of capital at stake, was the source of the risk-management institutions that still undergird the global economy today. A key institution of early modern risk management was General Average, a procedure used to redistribute extraordinary cos
content.speaker: New Books Network

content.title: Timothy McCall, "Making the Renaissance Man: Masculinity in the Courts of Renaissance Italy" (Reaktion Books, 2023)
content.description:
Looking beyond the marble elegance of Michelangelo's David, the pugnacious, passionate, and--crucially--important story of Renaissance manhood. 
Timothy McCall's book Making the Renaissance Man: Masculinity in the Courts of Renaissance Italy (Reaktion, 2023) explores the images, objects, and experie
content.speaker: New Books Network

content.title: Marissa Nicosia, "Shakespeare in the Kitchen" (Routledge, 2026)
content.description: Audiences and scholars alike have long remarked that Shakespeare’s poems and plays record the pleasures and perils of the table. Shakespeare in the Kitchen (Routledge, 2026) by Dr. Marissa Nicosia asks what Shakespeare’s works can tell us about Renaissance culinary recipes, and what these recipes ca
content.speaker: New Books Network

liveSessions.title: Live Q&A: Suffering & Sovereignty
liveSessions.description: Open mic. Ask Pastor Anil anything about suffering.

liveSessions.title: Youth Debate: Faith vs Science
liveSessions.description: Two youth leaders and a scientist walk through Genesis 1.

prayers.text: Please pray for my mother's surgery on Friday. That the Lord would guide the surgeon's hands.
Job interview tomorrow. Praying for peace and clarity — and that God closes or opens the right door.
GOD ANSWERED! After 8 months of waiting, my brother came to church with me. Praise Him!
Struggling with anxiety this week. Pray for the peace that surpasses understanding.
Pray for our church plant in Warangal. We need a worship leader and more monthly support.
Healed of stage-3 cancer — 5 years clear now. Jesus is the same yesterday, today, forever.

chatMessages.text: Welcome everyone — questions in the queue will be answered in order.
Pastor, how do we reconcile God's sovereignty with our pain?
Great question — let's turn to Romans 8:28 first.
Glory to God for this time.
